claim: In the Thornburg 2026 4D whole-cell model of JCVI-Syn3A, approximately 34%% of RNA polymerase holoenzymes (63 of 187 on average) are actively transcribing early in the cell cycle; the remainder transiently bind / unbind genes.
source_detail: Reported as an emergent prediction of the 4DWCM spatial simulation, not a directly-measured experimental value. Arises from RDME diffusion + gene-binding kinetics in the spatial model.
caveats: This is a SIMULATED value from Thornburg 2026's 4DWCM, not an experimental measurement. Confidence is 'inferred' pending direct measurement (e.g. single-molecule tracking of RNAP in live Syn3A).
Fraction likely varies across cell-cycle phase and from cell to cell; 0.34 is a mean early-cycle value.
Extracted from WebSearch snippets in Session 11.
